gold.rationale: The article's main idea is grounded in real WrestleMania-week activity and authentic fan/creator posts, but it somewhat overstates how unified and coherent that ecosystem was. It also uses related but not identical visuals in a way that can make readers treat magazine/editorial and fan-made content as if they were direct evidence of the immediate WrestleMania event environment.

atomic_points.post_summary: The post describes how WrestleMania in Las Vegas has evolved into a citywide entertainment festival driven by fan predictions, creator content, and crossover media, extending the event's impact far beyond the official stadium shows.

sentence_level.sentence_text: On WrestleMania weekend in Las Vegas, the mood announces itself almost immediately.
atoms: WrestleMania weekend took place in Las Vegas.
The atmosphere of the event was evident immediately.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In the airport arrivals hall, fans step off planes with replica championship belts looped over carry-ons, vintage wrestling T-shirts peeking out from under light jackets, and conversations already deep into fantasy finishes before anyone has made it to baggage claim.
atoms: Fans arrived at the airport arrivals hall in Las Vegas.
Fans carried replica championship belts on their luggage.
Fans wore vintage wrestling T-shirts.
Fans discussed fantasy match outcomes before collecting baggage.

sentence_level.sentence_text: By the time rideshares are moving past the Strip toward Allegiant Stadium, the city is doing its part too: giant screens flash fight ads, residency promotions, sports-betting pitches and luxury branding in the same visual dialect of spectacle.
atoms: Fans traveled via rideshare from the Strip to Allegiant Stadium.
Giant screens displayed fight advertisements.
Giant screens displayed residency promotions.
Giant screens displayed sports-betting pitches.
Giant screens displayed luxury branding.
Allegiant Stadium hosted the event.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Wrestling does not feel like a visiting attraction dropped into Las Vegas for a few days.
atoms: Wrestling feels integrated into Las Vegas rather than a temporary attraction.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It feels absorbed into the city's natural appetite for excess, then somehow turned up another notch.
atoms: Wrestling is described as fitting the city's appetite for excess.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That helps explain why WrestleMania in Las Vegas felt larger than a two-night stadium show, even with Saturday and Sunday still serving as the official center of the weekend.
atoms: WrestleMania in Las Vegas felt larger than a two-night show.
Saturday and Sunday were the official center of the weekend.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In the run-up, the announced WrestleMania card spread everywhere online.
atoms: The announced WrestleMania card spread online before the event.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans passed around night-by-night match boards, title predictions and mock winner graphics as if they belonged to the event's official marketing campaign.
atoms: Fans shared night-by-night match boards.
Fans shared title predictions.
Fans shared mock winner graphics.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Saturday and Sunday lineups became a kind of social currency, screenshotted into group chats, debated on livestreams and marked up with circles, arrows and all-caps reactions.
atoms: Saturday and Sunday lineups were screenshotted into group chats.
Saturday and Sunday lineups were debated on livestreams.
Fans marked up lineups with circles, arrows, and all-caps reactions.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In a city built on anticipation, wrestling fans arrived carrying a lot of their own.
atoms: Wrestling fans arrived with high anticipation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: By early April, the lineup had settled into a familiar WrestleMania shape: a two-night card broad enough to act as both payoff and reset.
atoms: The lineup settled by early April.
The event was a two-night card.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One of the most discussed officially announced matches was the Intercontinental Championship Ladder Match featuring Penta, Je'Von Evans, Dragon Lee, JD McDonagh, Rusev and Rey Mysterio.
atoms: The Intercontinental Championship Ladder Match was a discussed match.
Penta participated in the match.
Je'Von Evans participated in the match.
Dragon Lee participated in the match.
JD McDonagh participated in the match.
Rusev participated in the match.
Rey Mysterio participated in the match.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It was exactly the kind of multi-man collision that invites instant prediction graphics and endless fantasy-booking threads.
atoms: The match format invites prediction graphics.
The match format invites fantasy-booking threads.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Around the biggest title bouts, the conversation quickly moved beyond what had already been announced and toward what the outcomes might mean by Monday morning, on the road to Backlash, and in the wider post-Mania pecking order.
atoms: Conversation moved toward outcomes by Monday morning.
Conversation considered the road to Backlash.
Conversation considered the post-Mania pecking order.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The wrestling internet has a weakness for brackets, templates and hypotheticals, and WrestleMania week is when that tendency really flowers.
atoms: The wrestling internet uses brackets, templates, and hypotheticals.
WrestleMania week amplifies this tendency.

sentence_level.sentence_text: At least one widely shared social post laid out a full Saturday-and-Sunday prediction board, arranging marquee matches into a neat visual schedule and marking projected winners with circles and highlights.
atoms: A widely shared social post featured a Saturday-and-Sunday prediction board.
The board arranged marquee matches visually.
The board marked projected winners with circles and highlights.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It did not read like a casual opinion tossed off in passing.
atoms: The prediction post was not a casual opinion.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It looked more like a fan-built control-room display for the weekend ahead.
atoms: The post resembled a fan-built control-room display.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That matters, because modern wrestling fandom is deeply visual.
atoms: Modern wrestling fandom is described as deeply visual.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The appearance of certainty can travel almost as effectively as certainty itself.
atoms: Visual certainty travels effectively in fandom.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A polished prediction board moves faster through a feed than a dense paragraph of analysis, especially during a week when people are scrolling between flights, autograph sessions, convention stops and late-night shows.
atoms: Prediction boards move faster than analysis paragraphs.
Fans scrolled between flights, autograph sessions, conventions, and shows.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The same impulse drove another major lane of Mania-week content: projected championship landscapes.
atoms: Projected championship landscapes were a major content lane.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One fan-edit account circulated images showing who might leave Las Vegas with the company's major titles, turning post-WrestleMania status into something like a collectible mood board.
atoms: A fan-edit account circulated images of potential title winners.
Post-WrestleMania status was treated like a collectible mood board.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Belts were placed on likely winners.
atoms: Images placed belts on likely winners.

sentence_level.sentence_text: New champion lineups were arranged as if the next era had already been photographed and approved.
atoms: New champion lineups were arranged visually.

sentence_level.sentence_text: WrestleMania week compresses emotion and speculation into a strange accelerated timeline, where outcomes are debated before entrances are even timed.
atoms: WrestleMania week compresses emotion and speculation.
Outcomes are debated before entrances are timed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In that environment, graphics stop being decorative.
atoms: Graphics serve a functional purpose beyond decoration.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They become a way for fans to rehearse the future, to make a title change feel half-real once enough people have shared the image.
atoms: Graphics allow fans to rehearse future outcomes.
Shared images make title changes feel real.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That says a lot about the culture now surrounding WrestleMania.
atoms: The post comments on the culture surrounding WrestleMania.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Watching the matches and reacting after the bell no longer feels like the whole experience.
atoms: Watching matches and reacting is no longer the full experience.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans forecast the weekend, package it, publish it and circulate their own versions of it in real time.
atoms: Fans forecast the weekend.
Fans package and publish their own versions in real time.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Prediction posts function as mini-columns.
atoms: Prediction posts function as mini-columns.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fantasy champion grids become unofficial posters for the next season.
atoms: Fantasy champion grids act as unofficial posters.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Video creators build full weekly schedules around the event, and the tempo of WrestleMania week starts to resemble a media festival as much as a wrestling show.
atoms: Video creators build weekly schedules around the event.
WrestleMania week resembles a media festival.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One creator's WrestleMania-week schedule captured that change neatly.
atoms: A creator's schedule exemplified the change.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Tied to the Las Vegas slate, it rolled out a complete menu of related programming: a prediction show on Thursday, a watch-thon built around archival WWE material, a SmackDown review on Friday, a Hall of Fame watch-along later that night, then separate live reactions and review streams for WrestleMania Saturday and WrestleMania Sunday.
atoms: A prediction show aired on Thursday.
A watch-thon used archival WWE material.
A SmackDown review aired on Friday.
A Hall of Fame watch-along aired Friday night.
Live reactions aired for WrestleMania Saturday.
Review streams aired for WrestleMania Sunday.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The point was not simply that fans would watch WrestleMania.
atoms: The goal was more than just watching the matches.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It was that many of them would spend the entire week inside a continuous content loop, moving from preview to nostalgia to recap to postgame without ever really leaving the same ecosystem.
atoms: Fans spent the week in a continuous content loop.
The loop moved from preview to nostalgia to recap to postgame.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Across Mania week, creator media increasingly fills the gaps between the official tentpoles and often shapes how the event gets processed in the moment.
atoms: Creator media fills gaps between official events.
Creator media shapes how the event is processed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That expanded itinerary has become one of the defining features of Mania week in any host city, and Las Vegas may be the cleanest fit yet.
atoms: The expanded itinerary is a defining feature of Mania week.
Las Vegas is described as a clean fit for this model.

sentence_level.sentence_text: WWE's official calendar included WrestleMania at Allegiant Stadium, Friday Night SmackDown and the Raw after WrestleMania at T-Mobile Arena, along with the multi-day WWE World fan experience at the Las Vegas Convention Center South Hall.
atoms: WrestleMania was at Allegiant Stadium.
Friday Night SmackDown was on the official calendar.
Raw after WrestleMania was at T-Mobile Arena.
The WWE World fan experience was at the Las Vegas Convention Center South Hall.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Hall of Fame ceremony added another formal destination and gave the week a clear branded backbone from Thursday through Monday.
atoms: The Hall of Fame ceremony was a formal destination.
The week ran from Thursday through Monday.

sentence_level.sentence_text: But the backbone was only part of the story.
atoms: The official schedule was only part of the story.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Around it, fan conventions, creator appearances and adjacent wrestling events made the map denser.
atoms: Fan conventions, creator appearances, and adjacent events made the map denser.

sentence_level.sentence_text: WrestleCon ran at the Horseshoe Hotel & Casino, drawing collectors, regulars on the meet-and-greet circuit and the kind of nostalgia-driven foot traffic that can turn hallways and casino floors into improvised reunion spaces.
atoms: WrestleCon ran at the Horseshoe Hotel & Casino.
WrestleCon drew collectors and regulars.
WrestleCon drew nostalgia-driven foot traffic.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Independent wrestling around the city added more movement after dark and between the larger shows, creating that familiar Mania-week feeling that somewhere in town another ring is always being set up.
atoms: Independent wrestling occurred after dark.
Independent wrestling occurred between larger shows.
The feeling was that another ring was always being set up.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For traveling fans, the line between official and adjacent often matters less than the sensation of total immersion.
atoms: Traveling fans prioritize total immersion over the official/adjacent distinction.

sentence_level.sentence_text: If it is on the calendar, reachable by rideshare and filled with the same crowd, it becomes part of the same weekend.
atoms: Events on the calendar, reachable by rideshare, and filled with the same crowd become part of the weekend.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That perspective came through clearly in a creator post from Jazzy Yang, who treated Mania Week less as a single destination than as a citywide route.
atoms: Jazzy Yang posted about Mania Week.
Jazzy Yang treated Mania Week as a citywide route.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The post read like a practical guide crossed with a hype reel, asking followers what TikTok content should get made this time and mapping out where to find her during the week.
atoms: The post asked followers about TikTok content ideas.
The post mapped out where to find Jazzy Yang.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It was casual, playful and built for social media, but it also captured something essential about the way wrestling now moves through a host city.
atoms: The post was casual and playful.
The post captured how wrestling moves through a host city.

sentence_level.sentence_text: WrestleMania week is increasingly navigated through personality-driven schedules: who is going live and where, which watch party is worth dropping into, which meetup is turning into its own scene, which appearance or panel might generate the week's most widely shared clip.
atoms: WrestleMania week is navigated through personality-driven schedules.
Fans decide which watch parties to attend.
Fans decide which meetups to attend.
Fans look for appearances or panels to generate clips.

sentence_level.sentence_text: This is the Las Vegas version of wrestling's modern sprawl.
atoms: This represents the Las Vegas version of wrestling's modern sprawl.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The city does not just host the headliner.
atoms: The city hosts more than just the headliner.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It becomes a temporary network of overlapping fan routes.
atoms: The city becomes a network of overlapping fan routes.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Someone might spend the morning at a convention signing, the afternoon filming content near the Strip, the evening at SmackDown or a side event, and the night back in a hotel room arguing over title outcomes on social media.
atoms: Fans might spend mornings at convention signings.
Fans might spend afternoons filming content near the Strip.
Fans might spend evenings at SmackDown or side events.
Fans might spend nights arguing over title outcomes on social media.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Someone else might build the week around creator panels, meetups and livestream communities more than the in-ring action itself.
atoms: Some fans build the week around creator panels, meetups, and livestreams.

sentence_level.sentence_text: WrestleMania remains the anchor, but attending it now means inhabiting an experience that extends well beyond bell to bell.
atoms: WrestleMania remains the anchor.
The experience extends beyond the matches.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That expansion into a broader culture shows up in the images that circulate during the week as much as in the events themselves.
atoms: Circulating images show the cultural expansion.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One of the more striking crossover visuals in the atmosphere around WrestleMania featured Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posing for Paper magazine.
atoms: Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posed for Paper magazine.
The shoot was a crossover visual.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The shoot was glossy, styled and clearly designed for fashion-media circulation.
atoms: The shoot was glossy and styled.
The shoot was designed for fashion-media circulation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It underscored how easily wrestling personalities now move between storyline television and editorial spaces that once might have treated the industry as too niche or too camp to spotlight seriously.
atoms: Wrestling personalities move between TV and editorial spaces.

sentence_level.sentence_text: During Mania week, those categories blur even more.
atoms: Categories blur during Mania week.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A magazine image, a fan-made champion graphic and a promotional match card can sit side by side in the same feed and contribute to the same overall sense of event.
atoms: Magazine images, fan graphics, and match cards appear together in feeds.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That shift matters because WrestleMania has increasingly become a lifestyle story as much as a sports-entertainment one.
atoms: WrestleMania has become a lifestyle story.

sentence_level.sentence_text: There is still the stadium scale, still the familiar attention to title changes and legacy matches.
atoms: The stadium scale remains.
Attention to title changes and legacy matches remains.

sentence_level.sentence_text: But there is also the fashion logic of wrestler presentation, the social-media afterlife of every entrance outfit, and the editorial framing of certain stars as celebrity figures rather than simply members of the roster.
atoms: Fashion logic applies to wrestler presentation.
Entrance outfits have a social-media afterlife.
Stars are framed as celebrity figures.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio appearing in a glossy magazine spread during the same season as WrestleMania coverage reinforces the point.
atoms: Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio appeared in a magazine spread during WrestleMania season.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Wrestling now operates in a wider pop-cultural register.
atoms: Wrestling operates in a wider pop-cultural register.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It is talked about through ring work and storylines, but also through aesthetics, chemistry, image and trend value.
atoms: Wrestling is discussed through aesthetics, chemistry, image, and trend value.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Las Vegas amplifies all of it.
atoms: Las Vegas amplifies these trends.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It is a city that understands packaging, understands premium experience and understands how to stretch an event into an atmosphere that lasts all week.
atoms: Las Vegas understands packaging and premium experiences.
Las Vegas stretches events into a week-long atmosphere.

sentence_level.sentence_text: So the WrestleMania buildup there naturally spilled beyond wrestling-first spaces.
atoms: The buildup spilled beyond wrestling-first spaces.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans showed up ready to compare cards, but also ready to document outfits, scout activations, chase side events and gather around watch-alongs.
atoms: Fans documented outfits.
Fans scouted activations.
Fans chased side events.
Fans gathered around watch-alongs.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Social posts imagining post-Mania champions landed beside creator schedules.
atoms: Social posts about post-Mania champions appeared alongside creator schedules.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Prediction boards sat next to convention snapshots.
atoms: Prediction boards appeared next to convention snapshots.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Editorial images of WWE talent circulated as part of the same mood board.
atoms: Editorial images of WWE talent circulated in the mood board.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The citywide WrestleMania takeover was built out of all of it at once.
atoms: The citywide takeover was built from these elements.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Longtime fans will recognize something familiar in that, because WrestleMania has always inspired a kind of pilgrimage behavior.
atoms: WrestleMania has always inspired pilgrimage behavior.

sentence_level.sentence_text: People travel. They collect memories. They organize entire weekends around one major show.
atoms: People travel for the event.
People collect memories.
People organize weekends around the show.

sentence_level.sentence_text: What has changed is the density of everything around it.
atoms: The density of surrounding elements has changed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The fan ecosystem now produces its own media at industrial speed.
atoms: The fan ecosystem produces media at industrial speed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In a single week you get prediction streams, watch parties, convention content, outfit posts, reaction videos, recap podcasts and glossy photo packages.
atoms: A single week includes prediction streams, watch parties, convention content, outfit posts, reaction videos, recap podcasts, and photo packages.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Because all of that unfolds publicly and in parallel, the event starts to feel bigger than its official runtime.
atoms: The event feels bigger than its official runtime due to parallel public unfolding.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That may be the most useful way to think about WrestleMania in 2026.
atoms: The post suggests this perspective for WrestleMania in 2026.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It is not merely WWE's annual premium live event, large as that centerpiece remains.
atoms: WrestleMania is not merely a premium live event.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It makes more sense now as an entertainment festival with a wrestling ring at the center.
atoms: WrestleMania is an entertainment festival with a wrestling ring at the center.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The stadium card supplies the stakes.
atoms: The stadium card supplies the stakes.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The official WWE schedule provides the architecture.
atoms: The official WWE schedule provides the architecture.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The citywide energy comes from fan graphics, creator itineraries, convention traffic, independent wrestling movement and crossover images that place wrestling talent inside fashion and culture media.
atoms: Citywide energy comes from fan graphics.
Citywide energy comes from creator itineraries.
Citywide energy comes from convention traffic.
Citywide energy comes from independent wrestling movement.
Citywide energy comes from crossover images.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In Las Vegas, WrestleMania week became a full-spectrum occupation of attention, one that began before the first entrance theme hit and lingered well after the final review stream ended.
atoms: WrestleMania week occupied attention fully.
The attention began before the first entrance theme.
The attention lingered after the final review stream.

sentence_level.sentence_text: And on the Strip, where every property is trying to persuade you that the next attraction is the biggest one in town, that may be the most WrestleMania outcome imaginable.
atoms: Properties on the Strip compete for attention.
This outcome fits the WrestleMania theme.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For a few charged days, the wrestling world does not ask to be treated like a sideshow.
atoms: The wrestling world does not want to be treated like a sideshow.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It carries itself like the main event across the entire city.
atoms: The wrestling world carries itself like the main event across the city.

paragraph_level: WrestleMania in Las Vegas has evolved from a two-night stadium show into a citywide entertainment festival.
The event's atmosphere is driven by fan-generated content, including prediction boards, fantasy graphics, and social media speculation.
Creator media and personality-driven schedules now fill the gaps between official WWE events, shaping how fans experience the week.
WrestleCon and independent wrestling events have densified the map, creating a sense of total immersion for traveling fans.
Wrestling personalities are increasingly treated as celebrities, appearing in fashion media and blurring the lines between sports entertainment and lifestyle.
Las Vegas amplifies this expansion by packaging the event as a week-long premium experience that extends beyond the ring.
In 2026, WrestleMania is best understood as an entertainment festival with a wrestling ring at its center, occupying attention from before the first entrance to after the final review.
The wrestling world now carries itself like the main event across the entire city rather than as a sideshow.
retrieval_queries: Las Vegas Convention Center South Hall

cross_modal: image_1 is presented as visual evidence of Liv Morgan's crossover into fashion media during WrestleMania week
image_2 is used to illustrate the fan-made prediction boards and match cards that circulated online during the event
image_3 is offered as an example of the official WrestleMania card that fans screenshot and debate

image_5 is shown as a fan-edit graphic projecting Liv Morgan as a potential post-WrestleMania champion
image_6 is presented as a promotional image for the Roman Reigns vs. CM Punk World Heavyweight Championship match

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to show how wrestling stars like Liv Morgan appear in fashion media (Paper magazine) during Mania week, blurring lines between sports and pop culture.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A glossy magazine cover featuring Liv Morgan in a red outfit holding a spiked weapon, with the title 'PAPER' and her name in stylized text.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image perfectly matches the text's description of a 'glossy, styled' magazine shoot featuring Liv Morgan, reinforcing the crossover narrative.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to exemplify the 'fan-built control-room display' or prediction boards that fans created and shared during the weekend.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A collage of WrestleMania Saturday match cards with white circles drawn around specific wrestlers (e.g., Cody Rhodes, Liv Morgan, Seth Rollins, AJ Lee).
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image aligns with the text's description of fans marking up match cards with circles and highlights to make predictions.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to show the official WrestleMania Sunday card, which fans debated and used as a basis for their own predictions.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A promotional poster for WrestleMania Sunday featuring matches like Roman Reigns vs. CM Punk, Bayley vs. Rhea Ripley, and the 6-man tag match.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image provides the official visual for the Sunday card mentioned in the text, grounding the fan discussions in official material.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post cites this image as a 'creator post' by Jazzy Yang that served as a practical guide and hype reel for Mania Week activities.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A stylized collage featuring Jazzy Yang in a pink digital camera frame, alongside screenshots of 'Podermania' and 'Night Out 16' event posters.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image visually represents the 'personality-driven schedule' and 'citywide route' described in the text, featuring the named creator Jazzy Yang.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to illustrate the 'fan-edit account' that circulated projected championship landscapes, specifically showing Liv Morgan as a potential winner.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A dramatic portrait of Roman Reigns holding a WWE Championship belt, with red lightning effects in the background.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows Roman Reigns, but the text describes a fan edit showing Liv Morgan. The binding is slightly misaligned as the image does not show the specific fan edit described, but rather an official promo for a different wrestler.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to show the official WrestleMania Sunday card, which fans debated and used as a basis for their own predictions.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A promotional poster for WrestleMania Sunday featuring matches like Roman Reigns vs. CM Punk, Bayley vs. Rhea Ripley, and the 6-man tag match.

retrieved_evidence.text:
Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posing for @papermagazine latest edition. 

#WrestleMania

Liv Morgan’s appearance in a glossy Paper magazine editorial has been highlighted in recent reports as emblematic of how WrestleMania week in Las Vegas now extends deep into fashion and lifestyle culture. According to entertainment writers, the spread emphasizes bold styling, couture-inspired outfits and pose-heavy layouts that situate Morgan not just as a WWE performer but as a mainstream pop figure, aligning with the broader trend of wrestling personalities making high-fashion media appearances around the city as fans arrive for WrestleMania.

A fan-edit account focused on wrestling content has been posting stylized images imagining which performers might leave WrestleMania weekend holding major championships, according to recent uploads. The edits depict various stars posing with title belts, sometimes in alternate color schemes or in front of Las Vegas-themed backdrops, suggesting different scenarios for how the main events could end. Followers are responding by proposing their own preferred champions, turning the account into a focal point for speculation about the direction of the title scene.
#WrestleMania predictions!

As WrestleMania approaches, observers note that the surrounding week has developed into a multi-layered festival that extends well beyond the main stadium events. According to industry watchers and fan organizers, the period now includes formal WWE programming, expansive fan media coverage, convention-style gatherings and a busy calendar of independent wrestling shows across the city. This wider ecosystem, often described informally as WrestleMania week, is seen as drawing visitors for both official attractions and numerous adjacent activities concentrated around the host area.

A collage-style promotional graphic for “Mania Week” circulating from Las Vegas has been described as bringing together a dense itinerary of creator appearances, independent side cards and themed nightlife tied loosely to WWE’s presence. According to event organizers, such layouts help coordinate meetups for podcasters, streamers and local wrestlers, listing venues, times and featured personalities so that visiting fans can treat WrestleMania week as a roaming festival of wrestling-related happenings beyond the official stadium shows.

A widely circulated post featuring Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio is drawing attention to how prominent wrestling personalities are increasingly appearing in glossy editorial-style spaces outside the ring, according to observers of recent coverage. The images, which resemble fashion or lifestyle photo spreads, place the performers in carefully styled outfits and settings rather than in-ring gear. Commenters suggest such appearances may help broaden their mainstream visibility and connect with audiences who might not regularly follow weekly wrestling programming.

Alongside the unofficial match cards, fans and independent creators have begun publishing detailed prediction graphics outlining who they believe will win key bouts and which performers could emerge as champions after the weekend, according to online activity monitored this week. These visuals, often formatted like sports brackets or betting sheets, are being shared ahead of the event as conversation starters. Commenters are using them to debate likely outcomes, with particular focus on headline title matches and possible surprise changes in the championship picture.

A final set of auxiliary materials often returns to individual WWE personalities such as Dominik Mysterio making high-profile media or fashion-related appearances in Las Vegas during WrestleMania week. Entertainment reports describe Mysterio and other wrestlers attending red-carpet events, lifestyle interviews or branded parties, styling themselves in ways that bridge ring personas and mainstream celebrity culture, thereby cementing the week’s reputation as a pop-culture spectacle that extends well beyond the wrestling ring.

At least one widely shared post this week displays a complete two-night prediction board for WrestleMania, spanning both the Saturday and Sunday shows, according to early monitoring of social media content. The board lays out a full slate of marquee title matches and featured grudge bouts, with the anticipated winners clearly circled or highlighted. Users are treating the graphic as a comprehensive forecast for the entire weekend, using it to discuss which belts might change hands and how the results could influence storylines in the weeks following the event.
Post #WrestleMania champions?...what's your predictions 👀⬇️

Some supporting reports in recent WrestleMania week features have focused on independent promotions running parallel cards in smaller Las Vegas venues, showing compact arenas, intimate seating arrangements and local talent preparing for matches. According to wrestling industry observers, these accounts illustrate how Mania Week functions as an ecosystem where non-WWE shows benefit from the influx of visitors, giving rising performers and regional promotions access to larger audiences and networking opportunities.

Additional illustrative material has highlighted the technology-driven side of WrestleMania week, including mobile apps, AR filters and social platforms that fans use to share brackets, polls and live reactions from Las Vegas. Media commentators explain that these tools allow supporters to participate in prediction games, remix official content and follow creator-driven coverage in real time, reinforcing the perception of Mania Week as a hybrid digital-physical festival for wrestling enthusiasts.

Fan culture around WrestleMania week in Las Vegas increasingly revolves around prediction boards that outline the Saturday cards in detail, listing multiple title bouts and marking presumed winners with circles or notes. In recent accounts, these informal boards have been described as shared reference points that turn every championship match into a kind of participatory referendum, as supporters debate storylines, betting odds and booking patterns long before the first bell rings in the official WWE events.

Auxiliary materials associated with WrestleMania week coverage have often highlighted the broader Las Vegas backdrop, emphasizing neon-lit streets, packed casinos and branded fan zones that reinforce the idea of a citywide takeover. Media analysts note that these elements underscore how WWE and its partners integrate live programming with experiential marketing, blending sports entertainment with tourism and nightlife to attract both dedicated fans and casual visitors during the high-profile week.

By Sunday of WrestleMania week, attention in Las Vegas tends to focus on the top championship bouts that will define the post-Mania landscape, and prediction boards for the second night have reflected this emphasis. Commentators describe these boards as centering on the main titles, with annotations about potential heel turns, rematches and future feuds, as fans and creators speculate about who will carry WWE’s top belts and how the results might shape the company’s storytelling through the rest of the year.

MANIA WEEKK!!!!🥳🫶🫶✨🫦👀💚 
•
What TikTok should I make everyone do this time??👀🤔
•
This is where you can find me!
•
#maniaweek #wrestlemania2026 #wrestlemania #vegas2026 #jazzyyang

WrestleMania Sunday Watch Along + REVIEW

Other auxiliary accounts have centered on creator meetups and content hubs set up in Las Vegas hotel conference spaces or themed lounges, where podcasters, YouTubers and streamers gather with fans. Reports describe ring-inspired decor, branded backdrops and portable recording setups as common, reflecting how WrestleMania week has become a prime window for collaborative projects, live podcast tapings and social media coverage that amplify the event far beyond the main WWE broadcasts.

In recent days, online discussion around the upcoming WrestleMania has intensified as fans treat the show as a two-night event in Las Vegas, according to posts shared on major social platforms. Circulating material includes separate match cards for Saturday and Sunday, presented as tentative lineups for the stadium shows. While these cards are not officially confirmed schedules, observers note that the designs closely mirror recent storylines on weekly programming, and are being widely reposted in fan communities as a reference point for the expected structure of the weekend.

Another prominent fan post circulating in recent days is promoting a range of digital programming tied to WrestleMania week, including live watch-alongs, prediction streams and post-show review segments, according to creators involved in the campaign. The material encourages viewers to synchronize their viewing with online hosts, offering commentary and chat-based interaction during and after the event. Content producers say this type of programming has become a regular part of the build-up and aftermath, extending discussion of the matches beyond the broadcast itself.

One creator’s recent post presents so-called Mania Week as a broader itinerary that extends beyond the stadium shows, outlining a schedule of appearances, signings and side events around Las Vegas. According to the post, fans are being encouraged to view the week as a multi-day experience, combining official ticketed attractions with informal gatherings at venues across the city. The shared schedule highlights how performers, podcast hosts and other personalities are expected to move between media spots, fan meetups and late-night events.

Fan-crafted portraits imagining a favorite star emerging from WrestleMania in Las Vegas as a new champion have become a notable part of the online conversation, with one widely discussed example showing a performer holding prominently displayed WWE gold. In recent reports on digital fan culture, these creative works are framed as speculative storytelling tools, blending aesthetic flair with booking theories to visualize alternate outcomes, surprise cash-ins or long-awaited title wins during the citywide spectacle of WrestleMania week.

WrestleMania in Las Vegas has evolved from a two-night stadium show into a citywide entertainment festival.
The event's atmosphere is driven by fan-generated content, including prediction boards, fantasy graphics, and social media speculation.
Creator media and personality-driven schedules now fill the gaps between official WWE events, shaping how fans experience the week.
WrestleCon and independent wrestling events have densified the map, creating a sense of total immersion for traveling fans.
Wrestling personalities are increasingly treated as celebrities, appearing in fashion media and blurring the lines between sports entertainment and lifestyle.
Las Vegas amplifies this expansion by packaging the event as a week-long premium experience that extends beyond the ring.
In 2026, WrestleMania is best understood as an entertainment festival with a wrestling ring at its center, occupying attention from before the first entrance to after the final review.
The wrestling world now carries itself like the main event across the entire city rather than as a sideshow.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reflects the evidence regarding the citywide festival atmosphere, fan predictions, creator schedules, and the Liv Morgan/Dominik Mysterio magazine shoot, but it frames the fan-generated prediction boards and championship graphics as 'official marketing campaigns' or 'officially announced' elements, which constitutes a distortion of the source.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: Post claims fans shared match boards 'as if they belonged to the event's official marketing campaign' and that the 'announced WrestleMania card spread everywhere online', whereas evidence (src_374_11, src_374_6) clarifies these are 'not officially confirmed schedules' and 'unofficial match cards' created by fans and independent creators.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Fans passed around night-by-night match boards... as if they belonged to the event's official marketing campaign.
The announced WrestleMania card spread everywhere online.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is a high-quality cultural analysis that correctly identifies the shift towards a citywide festival and the role of fan-generated content, but it inaccurately attributes the fan-made prediction graphics to official WWE marketing, creating a distortion of the event's organizational reality.

images.scene_description: A high-fashion editorial photo of Liv Morgan with platinum blonde hair and a red outfit, holding a spiked weapon, set against a gritty industrial background. The image features large typography for 'PAPER' magazine and stylized text for her name.

images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite editorial layout with large overlaid text elements ('PAPER', 'LIV MORGAN') and a stylized font that are clearly added in post-production to create a magazine cover aesthetic.
images.explanation: This is a genuine Paper magazine editorial cover featuring Liv Morgan, consistent with the post's claim about her crossover into fashion media; the only distortion is the standard graphic design overlay of the magazine title and subject name.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post uses a mix of official graphics, fan annotations, and AI-generated images to illustrate the fan-driven nature of WrestleMania week. The images are generally consistent with the post's claims, with the main distortion being the AI-generated image of Roman Reigns.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管文章关于拉斯维加斯摔角狂热周演变为城市节日的核心描述是准确的，但文本中存在误导性扭曲，声称粉丝将比赛板分享得“仿佛属于官方营销活动”，这与证据不符。此外，图像显示明显的视觉编辑（如白色圆圈覆盖）和一张疑似AI生成的图像，这些视觉元素与文本中描述的粉丝制作图形相一致，但由于存在文本扭曲和视觉合成/编辑，整体真实性受到损害。
judge.reasoning_trace: Text has T2 distortion about official campaign framing. Images have V2 editing and V1 synthetic content. 3 L2 labels -> Mostly False.
judge.core_claim: WrestleMania in Las Vegas has evolved into a citywide entertainment festival driven by fan predictions, creator content, and crossover media, extending the event's impact far beyond the official stadium shows.
key_subclaims: Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posed for a glossy Paper magazine spread during WrestleMania week.
Fans shared night-by-night match boards and prediction graphics as if they belonged to the event's official marketing campaign.
A fan-edit account circulated images showing who might leave Las Vegas with major titles.
Jazzy Yang posted a creator itinerary for Mania Week across Las Vegas.
The Intercontinental Championship Ladder Match featured Penta, Je'Von Evans, Dragon Lee, JD McDonagh, Rusev, and Rey Mysterio.
Roman Reigns and CM Punk faced off in the World Heavyweight Championship Match.
WrestleCon ran at the Horseshoe Hotel & Casino.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posed for a glossy Paper magazine spread during WrestleMania week.
subclaim_findings.note: Social post confirms Liv Morgan and Dominik Mysterio posing for Paper magazine.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Fans shared night-by-night match boards and prediction graphics as if they belonged to the event's official marketing campaign.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms fans shared prediction graphics, but does not support the claim that they were shared 'as if they belonged to the official marketing campaign'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: A fan-edit account circulated images showing who might leave Las Vegas with major titles.
subclaim_findings.note: News snippet confirms a fan-edit account posted stylized images imagining performers holding major championships.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Jazzy Yang posted a creator itinerary for Mania Week across Las Vegas.
subclaim_findings.note: Social post from Jazzy Yang outlines a Mania Week itinerary and asks for TikTok content ideas.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The Intercontinental Championship Ladder Match featured Penta, Je'Von Evans, Dragon Lee, JD McDonagh, Rusev, and Rey Mysterio.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms a widely shared post displayed a complete two-night prediction board including this specific ladder match.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Roman Reigns and CM Punk faced off in the World Heavyweight Championship Match.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the World Heavyweight Championship match was a marquee bout on the Sunday card.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: WrestleCon ran at the Horseshoe Hotel & Casino.
subclaim_findings.note: News snippet mentions WrestleCon ran at the Horseshoe Hotel & Casino.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post's core claim about the citywide festival atmosphere is accurate and well-supported by evidence. However, the text contains a distortion (T2) regarding fan match boards being shared 'as if they belonged to the official marketing campaign,' which is not supported by evidence. Additionally, the images show clear visual editing (V2) with overlays and one image (05_img_5.jpg) that appears to be AI-generated (V1), representing fan-made graphics as described in the text. Since there are 3 L2 labels (T2, V2, V1) and no T1 or V1 alone, the verdict is Mostly False due to the combination of textual distortion and visual fabrication/editing.

image_findings.explanation: Image shows Liv Morgan in a Paper magazine cover, consistent with the text's claim of a crossover visual.
Image shows a promotional poster with white circles highlighting matches, consistent with the text's description of fan-edited prediction boards.
